name: stem_cell_clinic_overseas_cells_partner_hospital_liability
premise: CFO Elena Park must decide whether to renew a contract importing culture-expanded stem cells from an overseas clinic after an anonymous leak alleges incomplete chain-of-custody paperwork. Chief counsel Noah Feld warns continuing shipments could void malpractice reinsurance if a batch is traced to non-GMP conditions. Discontinuing cells cancels thirty-two scheduled cardiac regeneration trials for patients already immunosuppressed. The partner hospital CEO privately asks for loyalty until an onsite audit finishes next month.

agents.name: Elena Park
agents.identity: I am the health system chief financial officer balancing trial continuity, reinsurance covenants, and fiduciary duty when vendor documentation is questioned.
agents.goal: I want patients treated without signing balance sheets that implode if a batch is later recalled.

agents.name: Noah Feld
agents.identity: I am chief counsel interpreting FDA import pathways, sponsor agreements, and whistleblower obligations after the custody leak.
agents.goal: I want shipments paused until chain-of-custody is provably intact even if trials slip and public headlines accuse the system of cruelty.

choices: I will pause imports and switch patients to autologous fallback arms where ethically possible, accepting trial underpowering and patient disappointment.
I will continue shipments under escrow funding for rapid third-party audit with enhanced per-batch sequencing, accepting reinsurance breach risk if auditors find GMP gaps.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Trial sponsors need Elena Park's signature before the courier arrives at midnight. Elena Park must choose between pausing imports or continuing under escrow audit. What does Elena Park do?
